enonce.texte:
Match the words on the left with their definitions on the right.

A. Modern medicine1. Medical practices based on cultural traditions and natural remedies.
B. Traditional medicine2. Healthcare based on scientific research and advanced technology.
C. Alternative medicine3. A condition that persists over a long period.
D. Chronic disease4. Non-conventional approaches to healthcare.
E. Treatment5. The management and care of a patient to fight a disease.

questions.texte: Match A to its correct definition.
Match B to its correct definition.
Match C to its correct definition.
Match D to its correct definition.
Match E to its correct definition.

intitule: Listening Comprehension: A Discussion on Medicine

enonce.texte:
Listen to the following discussion (simulated or provided by the teacher) between a doctor and a patient about different medical approaches. Answer the questions below.

[Teacher's note: Read the following script aloud to students twice.]

Doctor: "Good morning. What brings you here today?"
Patient: "I've had joint pain for months. My grandmother says I should try traditional herbs, but I'm not sure."
Doctor: "Traditional medicine can be effective for some conditions. However, we first need to understand the cause of your pain. Modern medicine uses precise diagnostic tools to identify the problem."
Patient: "Is modern medicine always better?"
Doctor: "It is often more precise, but it can be expensive. Many people in Cameroon also use traditional remedies because they are accessible and affordable. Each approach has its place."
Patient: "What about alternative medicine like acupuncture?"
Doctor: "Some patients find it helpful for pain relief, but it's important to consult a qualified practitioner. The key is to make informed choices."

questions.texte: Identify the main reason why the patient visited the doctor.
questions.reponse: The patient has had joint pain for months.

questions.texte: Explain why the patient is hesitant about modern medicine.
questions.reponse: The patient is hesitant because modern medicine can be expensive.

questions.texte: Analyze the doctor's view on traditional medicine.
questions.reponse: The doctor acknowledges that traditional medicine can be effective for some conditions and is more accessible and affordable, but emphasizes the need for proper diagnosis.

questions.texte: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of modern medicine as mentioned in the dialogue.
questions.reponse: Advantage: More precise with better diagnostic tools. Disadvantage: Expensive.

intitule: Grammar Practice: Comparative and Superlative Forms

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the correct comparative or superlative form of the adjectives in parentheses.

questions.texte: Modern medicine is often ______ (precise) than traditional medicine.
Traditional medicine is usually ______ (accessible) than modern medicine in rural areas.
Alternative medicine is sometimes ______ (expensive) of the three options.
In Cameroon, modern medicine is considered ______ (effective) for emergency care.
For chronic diseases, patients often say that a combination of treatments is ______ (good) than a single approach.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences using comparative structures as instructed.

questions.texte: Modern medicine is precise. Traditional medicine is also precise but different in approach. (Rewrite using 'as...as')
questions.reponse: Modern medicine is as precise as traditional medicine, but their approaches are different.

questions.texte: Traditional medicine is affordable. Modern medicine is not as affordable. (Rewrite using a comparative form)
questions.reponse: Traditional medicine is more affordable than modern medicine.

questions.texte: Chronic diseases are treated in many ways. Some ways are more effective than others. (Rewrite using 'less...than')
questions.reponse: Some ways of treating chronic diseases are less effective than others.

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are part of a community discussion on healthcare choices. In a group of three, prepare a short debate or role-play where each person represents a different perspective:

  • Person A: Supports modern medicine only.
  • Person B: Believes in traditional medicine only.
  • Person C: Advocates for a combination of both.

Use persuasive language and comparative forms to support your arguments. Be prepared to present your debate to the class.

questions.texte: Prepare and present your role-play. You will be evaluated on vocabulary, grammar (comparatives), and persuasive language.
questions.reponse: A debate that uses vocabulary related to healthcare systems, comparative forms, and persuasive language.

enonce.texte:
Write a short article (about 200 words) for a school health magazine comparing modern and traditional medicine in Cameroon.

In your article, you should:

  • Describe the advantages and disadvantages of each approach.
  • Use comparative and superlative forms.
  • Give examples from the Cameroonian context.
  • Conclude with your opinion on the best approach.

questions.texte: Write the article using appropriate vocabulary and grammar.
questions.reponse: A well-structured article that uses vocabulary related to healthcare, comparative forms, and provides a balanced view with personal opinion.

intitule: Listening for Details: Advantages and Disadvantages

enonce.texte:
Listen again to the discussion (or read the transcript) and complete the table with the advantages and disadvantages of each approach mentioned.

ApproachAdvantagesDisadvantages
Modern Medicine1. 1.
Traditional Medicine1. 1.
Alternative Medicine1. 1.

questions.texte: List the advantages of modern medicine.
List the disadvantages of modern medicine.
List the advantages of traditional medicine.
List the advantages of alternative medicine.
questions.reponse: Helpful for pain relief for some patients.
